name: Division 819 Transit Employees
description_short: Member-owned credit union serving Amalgamated Transit Union Local 819 employees and families since 1945, offering savings accounts, loans, and online banking.
description_long: Division 819 Transit Employees Credit Union (D819TECU) was chartered in 1945 to serve members of Amalgamated Transit Union (ATU) Local 819, the largest ATU local in New Jersey representing over 2,000 active transit workers including operators, mechanics, and maintenance staff. The credit union has grown to serve 1,810+ members with assets exceeding $19.3 million and maintains a main office plus one branch location in the Irvington, New Jersey area. Currently led by Chairperson Shirley King, the organization operates as a nonprofit, member-owned cooperative where each member holds equal voting power and ownership stake. D819TECU offers standard credit union services including savings accounts with 24-hour online access, multiple loan products (new car loans, used car loans, and personal loans), direct deposit capabilities, and account management tools. Members can check balances, view transaction history, access their direct deposits, and manage passwords through secure online banking. The credit union operates a physical office at 186 Brookside Avenue in Irvington with limited hours (Mon-Fri 9am-4pm, transactions ending at 3pm), supplemented by digital account access for convenience. What distinguishes D819TECU is its explicit membership bond tied to Amalgamated Transit Union Local 819 employment, making it a specialized financial institution for a specific workforce community. The organization is governed democratically through annual member meetings where the membership elects a board of directors, which then selects officers and appoints a supervisory committee. This structure ensures direct accountability to members rather than external shareholders. The credit union emphasizes sound asset management and member input in service development. For consumers evaluating D819TECU, the primary consideration is membership eligibility—the credit union restricts services to ATU Local 819 employees and their immediate family members. While membership limitation provides community focus, it means the organization cannot serve the general public. The limited operating hours (no transactions after 3pm) and single branch location may create inconvenience for some members. Prospective members should verify current interest rates and loan terms directly, as these are referenced but not displayed on the website.
services: Savings accounts with secure online access
Checking accounts (implied through online banking and direct deposit capabilities)
24-hour online account access and balance checking
pros: Member-owned nonprofit structure with democratic governance—each member has one vote regardless of account balance
79-year operating history (since 1945) demonstrating stability and long-term commitment to the ATU Local 819 community
24-hour online banking access for account management, balance checking, and transaction history viewing
Multiple loan product offerings including new car, used car, and personal loans with online loan calculator tool
Membership open to immediate family of current ATU Local 819 members, extending access beyond just employees
Built-in community focus serving a specific workforce (2,000+ New Jersey Transit workers) with specialized financial understanding
Assets exceeding $19.3 million indicating sufficient size and stability for member protection
cons: Restricted membership eligibility limited to Amalgamated Transit Union Local 819 employees and immediate family only—not available to general public
Very limited operating hours with no transactions processed after 3pm weekdays, creating accessibility challenges for working members
Single branch location plus main office only, limiting in-person service options across New Jersey service area
Website does not display current interest rates or loan terms; members must contact office directly for pricing comparison
No mention of NCUA insurance coverage, FDIC equivalency, or other regulatory protections on public-facing website
best_for: New Jersey Transit workers (ATU Local 819 members) seeking stable financial services within their employment community
Family members of transit union employees looking for member-owned financial institution with democratic governance
Borrowers seeking car loans or personal loans with understanding from a lender familiar with transit worker income patterns
Savers prioritizing nonprofit, member-focused institutions over commercial banks or credit unions
diagnosis: D819TECU is best suited for Amalgamated Transit Union Local 819 employees and their immediate family members seeking a stable, member-owned financial institution with democratic governance and basic banking services. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—this credit union explicitly serves only transit union workers and their families, making it inaccessible to the general public regardless of creditworthiness or financial need.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
